subject: Chemistry
title: Nitrogen Triiodide: Touch and Boom
description: A 60-second lesson on Nitrogen Triiodide: Touch and Boom.

hook.text: Nitrogen triiodide is so sensitive that a feather's touch sets it off with a purple flash and a bang.

buildup.text: It's made by mixing iodine crystals with ammonia. When wet, it's harmless. When dry, it's terrifying.

discovery.text: The nitrogen-iodine bonds are extremely strained. Any vibration breaks them, releasing energy instantly.

twist.text: Chemistry teachers love it for demonstrations. Paint it on a surface, let it dry, and watch students jump.

climax.text: The purple cloud is iodine vapor. The loud crack comes from gas expanding faster than sound.

punchline.text: A feather sets it off. Purple smoke and a bang. Chemistry's best demo.

quiz.question: Why is nitrogen triiodide so sensitive to touch?
options: Its chemical bonds are extremely strained and break from any vibration
It absorbs moisture from skin and reacts
It contains radioactive isotopes that decay on contact
